lessons: Zod .default() fills partials — use .optional() + ?? in action body
app schema RPCs not in generated types — narrow-cast the rpc call
production_status REVOKE means schema must explicitly exclude it from update partials AND defense-in-depth strip at runtime
server-only package throws on import — vitest needs an alias to a no-op stub; Next.js still resolves the real one via react-server export condition
Schema column names matter — workstations.last_activity_at not last_heartbeat_at

rarv_cycles.scope_notes: PIN + record_scan_event wait for Phase 3 migrations

lessons: shop_settings lock invariant tested explicitly — timezone/currency/job_number_prefix cannot change after first job
supabase.rpc(name) defaults to public schema regardless of where the function actually lives — must use .schema('app').rpc() AND expose 'app' in supabase/config.toml [api].schemas

auth.admin.inviteUserByEmail does NOT accept app_metadata. Use createUser({app_metadata: {...}}) then generateLink({type: 'invite'}). The link_auth_user_to_actor trigger requires app_metadata.tenant_id at INSERT time on auth.users — without it the trigger raises auth_user_created_without_tenant_id.
PostgREST .or() with template-string concatenation is filter-injection prone. encodeURIComponent does NOT escape parens (RFC 3986 unreserved) — use explicit char map for ,():

rarv_cycles.scope_notes: Playwright E2E (Chromium) — list/create/detail/edit/packet lifecycle in describe.serial; auth fixture with E2E_STAFF_EMAIL/PASSWORD env vars
lessons: vitest exclude override loses defaults — must spread configDefaults.exclude before adding tests/e2e/** or zod's own tests in node_modules get collected (saw 2039-test run before fix)
Office sign-in fixture: page.waitForURL('**/dashboard') is the right success signal — sign-in action redirects to '/' which the office shell forwards to '/dashboard' via requireOfficeStaff

Brief-A 'safe parallel' files: tests/e2e/**, playwright.config.ts, vitest.config.ts (additive only), .gitignore (additive only) — no src/** writes keeps the brief composable with companies/portal briefs running concurrently

rarv_cycles.scope_notes: Single Server Component at src/app/(portal)/my/[jobId]/page.tsx — getMyJob + getCustomerVisibleTimeline; tsc clean, 161/161 vitest pass, zero regressions
lessons: Pre-existing parallel-route conflicts at /(office) vs /(portal) (both resolving '/', '/jobs', '/sign-in') block pnpm build but do NOT block strict child routes like /my/[jobId] — TypeScript + tests are the only verification gates that work until a host-split reconciliation brief lands

Customer-facing label rendering: import PRODUCTION_LABEL from '@/modules/jobs' (already on barrel) keeps office and portal status text consistent without duplicating the map; falling back to humanize(snake_case) is enough when a label is missing

TimelineEvent shape includes shop_employee_id / workstation_id / duration_seconds even on the customer query — query filters customer_visible=true server-side, but the type still surfaces the columns. UI must explicitly NOT render them; type system won't catch that omission, only review will.

rarv_cycles.scope_notes: 16 files in src/app/(office)/settings/ — tab nav layout, staff list/invite/edit/deactivate, workstations list+create panel with enrollment_url copy, shop settings with is_first_job_created lock invariant. scanner.tsx IScannerControls fix + scanning barrel split (lookupJobByPacketToken as Server Action, listShopEmployees removed from barrel). pnpm build green — 22 routes including all 5 settings routes.
lessons: IScannerControls.stop() not BrowserQRCodeReader.reset() — decodeFromVideoDevice returns IScannerControls; store the return value and call controls?.stop() in the useEffect cleanup. The codeReader itself has no stop/reset surface in zxing-browser.

Server-only barrel pollution: a module index.ts that has ANY re-export from a file with `import 'server-only'` poisons the barrel for ALL client component imports, even 'use server' action files in the same barrel. Fix: route server-only queries through a 'use server' action wrapper, OR remove them from the barrel and import directly in Server Components.

disabled inputs do NOT submit with FormData — HTML spec. When a field must be locked server-side but its saved value needs to be preserved on submit, either: (a) read the locked value server-side from DB in the action (ignore FormData for locked fields), or (b) render a hidden input alongside the disabled visible one. Approach (a) is safer (prevents tamper).
CopyLinkBox: navigator.clipboard.writeText is only available in secure contexts (https/localhost). For iPad PWA on local network this works fine; no fallback needed for this use-case.

enrollment_url security: the createWorkstation action must only return enrollment_url + workstation.name in its state — never the device_token. The device_token is a one-time secret; the enrollment_url embeds it but is the intended shareable form.

useActionState(action, initialState) requires the action signature to be (prevState, formData) — the prev state is injected as first arg by React. When binding a staffId into a deactivate action, use .bind: `const bound = deactivateStaffAction.bind(null, staffId)` then pass bound to useActionState.

rarv_cycles.scope_notes: 9 files in src/app/(office)/companies/ — list / new / detail / edit + shared CompanyFormFields. UI-only; module API + 27 vitest tests already existed. tsc clean, 161/161 vitest pass, zero regressions. Build still blocked on pre-existing parallel-route conflicts (per pops_app_build.md memory).
lessons: typedRoutes manifest is generated at Next build/dev time — newly created /(office)/companies/* routes are unknown to tsc until a build runs, so Link href={`/companies/${id}`} fails type-check on the FIRST tsc after creation. Two paths: (a) run pnpm dev/build once to regenerate, (b) cast `as Route` like jobs/[id] does. Cast is safer + faster in single-cycle Loki dispatches.

macOS `mv source1 source2 dest_dir/` SILENTLY overwrites when two sources share a basename. Lost (office)/page.tsx by stashing both (office)/page.tsx and (portal)/page.tsx to one dir. Reconstructed via mem-search obs #16016 which had the file's intent. Rule: stash with distinct names (mv X dest/X-renamed) or move one at a time.

createCompany / updateCompany surface a generic 'Invalid input' from Zod safeParse failures — form-layer error UX is degraded. Future improvement: surface field-level errors via FormState['fieldErrors']. Out of scope here per 'no module edits' constraint.

Zod `email: z.string().email().nullish()` rejects empty string but accepts null/undefined. The `stringOrNull` / `formDataString` helpers MUST convert '' → null (create) or undefined (update). Empty-string passthrough breaks creation.

archiveCompany is one-way (sets archived_at = now(); no unarchive method exists). Brief explicitly deferred building an archive button until an unarchive path lands. Don't ship destructive irreversible actions without an undo.

patterns: Server Action template: 'use server' → Zod safeParse → require* guard → getCurrentClaims (if needed) → createClient → typed insert/update → logAuditEvent → return narrow row
Query template: import 'server-only' → Zod parse → require* guard → createClient → chained .from().select().eq().order().range() → throw on .error → return data ?? []
Test template: vi.mock the four shared deps (db/server, db/admin, auth-helpers, audit) → typed chain mock or makeChain helper for awaitable builders → use RFC4122 v4 UUIDs in fixtures
Module index.ts re-exports actions + queries + their input/output types

RPC routing template: createClient → (supabase as unknown as {schema: ...}).schema('app') → .rpc<TResult, TArgs>(fn, args). Generic type defaults TArgs=void must be specified explicitly when passing args.

Staff invite template: insert app row → admin.createUser({app_metadata: {tenant_id, intended_actor}}) → admin.generateLink({type: 'invite'}) → roll back app row only if createUser fails; never roll back if generateLink fails (auth user already exists).
User-input search template: Zod-validate q (max length) → escapeForOr(q) before embedding in .or() template strings
anti_patterns_caught: Mocked supabase.rpc/admin tests pass even when the real call path is wrong — assert the mockSchema('app') call shape AND the createUser app_metadata shape, not just that the wrapper returns the expected fixture.
Zod .default() inside a schema that's later .partial()'d for update inputs will silently include the default in patch payloads. Use .optional() and apply default in the action body.
Re-using a module's index.ts edit pattern without Read first — old comment text in scaffolds varies subtly (ATTACH/APPLY vs INVITE/CREATE etc.).
